#Text=Only Fools
#Text=It was nearly winter when Cullen saw his mother for the last time.
#Text=She’d been all bundled and frayed, all carefully tucked away shudders, all red rimmed teary smiles.
#Text=His family was proud of him, they’d all said, but none of them would meet his eyes.
#Text=A Templar
#Text=.
#Text=Their boy.
#Text=Branson had tried and failed to seem all hard edges and unmoving firm pressed lips, Cullen supposed he’d wanted to have faith that his big brother would return.
#Text=Templar’s were heroes, Cullen had reminded him.
#Text=Heroes always returned.
#Text=Branson had pressed a coin into his palm with clammy hands and told him it was for luck.
#Text=Cullen had to fight back a sudden burning in his eyes when he shoved the coin into his boot.
#Text=Mia had trembled, tears staining her cheeks.

#Text=She was never afraid to hide her emotions, where Cullen was facts and rules, she was full-fledged impulse and fire, and she cried so hard that day Cullen had worried it would put all the fires in her out.
#Text=“I’m so proud to call you my brother,” she’d whispered, but it didn’t feel like the whole of her words had made its way past her downturned lips.
#Text=Mia’s shoulders were heavy when she hugged him tightly to her chest.
#Text=He was nearly at her chins height.
#Text=His youngest sister, Rosalie, was too little to understand what was happening, but she’d heard that Cullen was leaving and hadn’t stopped sobbing since.
#Text=He tried very hard not to seem upset when she’d wrapped her chubby arms around his neck and slapped a sloppy kiss on his cheek.
#Text=It had all been rather spur of the moment, he surmised, but the glint of Templar had caught his eye and he knew in his heart that it had stuck.
#Text=He was stubborn, like Mia had always told him.
#Text=He was strong like Branson believed.
#Text=And he was going to be a hero.
#Text=Leaving his only home of thirteen years was easier than it should have been.
#Text=He didn’t look back once as he followed the dusty dirt road out of dusty, dirty, Honnleath and onto a new life.
#Text=Training was excruciating, in many ways, and far simpler than he’d expected in others.
#Text=The new recruits were expected to leave all possessions behind, to forget earthly belongings.
#Text=“Your faith in the Maker is all you need.”
#Text=Cullen had never grown up with much to begin with, his family wasn’t destitute by any means, but they were a working family.
#Text=Farmers and blacksmiths and anything at all really to get food on the table and the door on its hinges.
#Text=His few belongings amounted to a little boat he pushed around on the lake made of driftwood and a scrap of fabric, and a toy sword.
#Text=He saw the holsters on the Templars hips, he knew of things better than what he had.
#Text=Yet, Cullen had kept the coin, tucked just inside the heel of his boot, and later in a sewn pocket on this inside of his lapel.
#Text=The Maker is all I need, he’d think, but he’d rub his thumbs over the grooves and divots and think of lakes and toy boats anyways.
#Text=As a working family, Cullen had partaken in a fair share of hard labour.
#Text=He was after all, the oldest son.
#Text=Traditions to uphold and family honour and the like; all responsibilities he’d never taken lightly.

#Text=Morning routines had him feeding and tending to horses, chickens, and their cow, heading off to help his father in the forge, tending to crops and digging up weeds; all manner of chores and hard work, little time for play.
#Text=The physical and material requirements, or lack thereof, were easy.
#Text=As if Cullen was meant to hold the Templar shield and sword proudly.
#Text=Faith in the Maker would see him through.
#Text=The emotional requirements, he’d found, he would struggle with for years.
#Text=As the years rolled on, and he was given more responsibilities and duties, he’d flourished.
#Text=His Knight-Captains told him sternly that he showed great promise and the Maker smiled upon his efforts.
#Text=He wore these gruff compliments with great pride, at first.
#Text=Cullen was growing taller, and his physical training and extreme labour resulted in a much tougher, angled, exterior.
#Text=His emotions, his tenderness, however, had yet to be moulded.
#Text=A little thumb grazed over a secretly kept coin, a little thought on watered smiles and plowed fields, a little boy from Honnleath stumbling his way into boots a little too big.
#Text=One day he’d been woken by a fellow Templar and herded off into his Knight-Captain’s office.
#Text=They’d spoke of promise and upholding the Maker’s will, and controlling abominations and dangerous mages, words and phrases he’d heard of, studied, but held no real meaning.
#Text=Mages were something of a mystery to him; all flickering light and dancing ice and terrible, terrible danger.
#Text=He supposed he was a bit fascinated at the time.
#Text=His Knight-Captain had clapped him on the shoulder, abruptly.
#Text=“You’re ready,” he’d said.
#Text=A glowing glass of blue liquid in his other hand.
#Text=Cullen knew of strength, he knew of righteous power and the bright blue courage Templar’s lived and breathed.
#Text=As he graciously accepted his gift, his reward and honour, he suddenly knew of order.
#Text=Lyrium never started out easily.
#Text=There were often fevers, uncontrollable shaking and hallucinations.
#Text=To a young boy of sixteen, born to common parents in a common town, magic hadn’t had the chance to so much flit past his vision.
#Text=It felt like fire, like ice.
#Text=It felt like liquid stars and burning embers.
#Text=For a long while, Cullen forgot who he was beyond the blur of blue and silver metal.
#Text=It would be the first of many, Templars weren’t introduced fully into lyrium until they underwent the ceremonial process at the age of eighteen, but this felt like something more.
#Text=Like a beginning.
#Text=“I serve the Maker and the Chantry and I will do as I am commanded.”
#Text=To serve the Maker.
#Text=And the Chantry.
#Text=And this power, this lyrium.
#Text=It felt like becoming a hero.
#Text=So he hefted his shield up a little more at an angle, better to deflect projectiles and acid and any other mage tricks, and swung his sword a little harder, a little faster.
#Text=In the quiet hours of the night, with the thrum of blue under his skin, he ran his thumb over the lucky coin one more time and thought of home.
#Text=“The Northern Free Marches,” Antoine, his Knight-Captain, began, “is a large sprawling mess of disorganized nature.
#Text=I despise it.”
#Text=He huffed.
#Text=“Yet, we are here, and it is by the Maker’s light that we travel.
#Text=We must all endure.”
#Text=Cullen said nothing, but quietly marvelled at the sheer amount of green around him.
#Text=Honnleath was a faded smudge of dust compared to the vibrancy of the forests here.
#Text=The air was so full, he felt as though his feet could gain wings.
#Text=The other new Templars shifted uneasily, restless.
#Text=They’d been on horseback for the majority of a week, the expanse of land available to them now had created an itch behind Cullen’s teeth.
#Text=He held himself very still.
#Text=It was his first actual mission, a month long endeavour to the Free Marches to gather materials for the Templars and investigate reports of strange magical phenomenon in the area.
#Text=He secretly believed the Captain had simply wanted to inspect the Templar camp nearby, Antoine was rather particular and a bit of a tattle tale, truth be told.
#Text=Cullen would never miss an opportunity for real world applications of his training, however, and tried his best to graciously accept the honour when it had been presented.
#Text=The forest was, enchanting in a strange sort of way.
#Text=There was an ancient feel to it, as if many eyes were upon him at all times, simply observing.
#Text=It made him twitchy, and terribly nervous.
#Text=At the same time, there was a great undercurrent of beauty and freedom here that he felt utterly enraptured by.
#Text=The first few nights he spent under the scattered stars peeking between the trees, and for the first time since he’d left home, he felt like crying.
#Text=An older Templar boy beside him snuffled in his sleep and mumbled something about fire and magic and Cullen slipped his coin into his hand and fell asleep with it tucked into a fist.
#Text=A week into his mission, Cullen found himself trampling through the tall grass and brush, alone.
#Text=He wasn’t sure how, but Roderick, the older Templar boy he’d become unsteady friends with, had managed to completely wander off without Cullen’s notice.

#Text=Cullen had stooped down to investigate a particularly strange coloured flower, attempting to ascertain whether it would be useful to bring clippings back to camp or if he’d develop a rash immediately after, and Roderick must have simply kept on walking.
#Text=How the older boy had made it this far into his life without wandering off a cliff, he’d never understand.
#Text=The boy could be distracted by a simple shadow.
#Text=To add to his misfortune, Cullen had ungracefully tripped over a branch shortly after and tumbled down quite a large hill.
#Text=In other words, he was rather lost.
#Text=A marvelous excursion, to say the least.
#Text=Antoine would be so proud.
#Text=He let out a long sigh, the hill he’d fell down was more like a cliff leading into a valley, it might be best to attempt to go around instead.
#Text=As he looked along the horizon with a sinking gut, he realized he would be walking for quite a while.
#Text=“Fantastic,” he muttered.
#Text=Cullen was aching from his morning training and it was rather hot, with a weighty humidity of late afternoon, so he picked a direction at random and began a sulking, steady trip onwards.
#Text=He’d been staring down at his boots, sulking- very un-Templar like, he was going for a record that day of the most mess ups- when suddenly there were a pair of brown feet in front of him.
#Text=“You’re going the wrong way,” a young voice told him, matter of fact.
#Text=He blinked, and there was a small girl in front of him.
#Text=Her short white hair was messily chopped and braided in strange patterns, and it fell in front of her large cat like eyes as she shook her head at him.
#Text=“Messy, messy shem.
#Text=Do you not know anything?”
#Text=It occurred to him that he should have been offended, but he couldn’t find it in him to close his mouth let alone think of a response.
#Text=Her words fired like daggers, belaying a quick, nimble quality underneath the tiny baby fat rounded cheeks.
#Text=She had markings…strange white ones that curled out from her hairline and onto her cheeks in a swirl of lines and circles.
#Text=Her eyebrows pulled together, in frustration or amusement he honestly could not decipher.
#Text=“Are you content to stand and blink like a toad, or shall I point you in the direction of your other metal men?”
#Text=He frowned in confusion, the wheels in his brain finally gaining traction.
#Text=“Metal…men?”
#Text=His words felt slow and stupid in comparison to her lightning quick ones.
#Text=She obviously thought the same, as her lips pressed together in a thin line.
#Text=“Yes, you are in fact wearing metal, are you not?”
#Text=She placed an impatient hand on her hip, and Cullen was half convinced he’d knocked himself out and was dreaming of the Fade.
#Text=Strange snarky girls wandering in the middle of the woods.
#Text=He opened his mouth to ask where in Maker’s breath she’d appeared from, if any of this was even real, just as she pushed a wayward strand of hair behind her ear.
#Text=Behind her long pointed ear.
#Text=“You’re an elf.”
#Text=She scoffed at him.
#Text=“And you, are Shemlen.”
#Text=She tossed him a dirty look and began nimbly stepping away.
#Text=“There’s a ledge just around the corner you can leap up.
#Text=Best be on your way.”
#Text=He stared dazedly after her, as she leapt onto a low branch and away from sight, disappearing almost as quickly as she’d appeared.

#Text=Cullen was unsure how long he’d stood in the clearing in confusion, but once he’d regained his senses he did find a ledge a few yards onwards he could pull himself up on, and promptly, the log he’d stumbled over before.
#Text=As he returned to camp a few hours later, covered in dirt and exhausted, he decided it best to pretend the strange Dalish girl had been simply a dream.
#Text=The alternative made him uneasy.
#Text=Tomorrow the troops were heading to a west camp, across the river.
#Text=He’d need his strength, and Maker preserve hi, he could use a break.
#Text=The eyes staring at him from the woods felt a little more solid that night, he found no comfort in the dead stars.
#Text=Cullen was floating, a drowsy pale light trailing towards him like outstretched hands.
#Text=A high pitched trill pulled at the back of his mind like an itch, a strange burning in his chest echoing along with it.
#Text=He couldn’t recall where he was, if he was awake.
#Text=He couldn’t find it in himself to much care, as his eyelids drifted closed.
#Text=Cullen was forgetting something, he was forgetting.
#Text=And suddenly the drifting muted light went dark.
#Text=I’m so proud to call you my brother,
#Text=Mia said, flipping a gold coin into the air.
#Text=So proud, Cullen.
#Text=But you need to wake up
#Text=.
#Text=“Wake up!”
#Text=He sputtered back into life with an abruptness that ached.
#Text=Like the grips of the Fade were unwilling to surrender such an easy voyager.
#Text=She was here again, all white cropped hair and large eyes.
#Text=She was saying something, using that word again, what was it?
#Text=Shen?
#Text=Shorn?
#Text=He wondered what it meant, and why she kept directing at him.
#Text=She had beautifully full lips, he noted blankly, as her mouth moved in speech he did not have the capacity to understand.
#Text=He felt hands on him, flittering about like birds, and thought maybe he groaned, or else the very earth below him shook apart.
#Text=A crevice in the dirt for him to float gently down into seemed almost too inviting.
#Text=It felt like molasses, like the stumbling drunken men he’d seen leaving taverns, all bubbly and baby footed.
#Text=There was pain in his lungs, though, and some distant part of him reasoned that his lungs should not be feeling the way they did, and it would be best if he were to breathe.
#Text=Then he was coughing, and there were cool hands pressed to his neck and back and stroking through his hair.
#Text=It reminded him of Mia, with her gold flipping coin, and the thought made him shake.
#Text=He closed his eyes tightly together as the coughs subsided, and pulled his knees to his chest.
#Text=The still distantness of the pain was too intense, suddenly, too loud.
#Text=He felt fire in his chest, in his head, and ice everywhere else.
#Text=He was cold, terribly so.
#Text=“Here, drink this.”
#Text=The Dalish girl pushed something hot into his hands, a cup of some kind, he focused on trying to move his hands.
#Text=He fumbled with it for a moment, breathing in the scent of elfroot and something sweet.
#Text=It soothed his aching, raw throat and gave some clarity to his addled mind, and Cullen realized he was very, very tired.
#Text=“Who are you?”
#Text=He rasped, as he felt himself sway unsteadily from his half upright position.
#Text=She admonished him lightly, pushing him downwards onto something soft.
#Text=He went willingly, or he fell, both were the same.
#Text=“Rest, Shemlen.”
#Text=His eyes felt tied down, pulled with weights and he couldn’t fight it if he tried.
#Text=He dreamt that he was holding someone’s hand, that something gentle brushed across his brow, and then he didn’t dream at all.
#Text=Cullen had been missing for days when the Templars found him on the side of the river.
#Text=It had rained heavily the day of their departure westward, the rivers ran faster than they’d anticipated.
#Text=A younger boy, Michael, had nearly fallen into the rushing waters as they crossed over a log, saved only by Cullen’s quick reflexes and strong arm.
#Text=Unfortunately, as Cullen hefted the boy to safety, it had been at the expense of his own balance and he’d tumbled unceremoniously into the waters head first.
#Text=They’d thought him dead.
#Text=By all accounts, Cullen should have died.
#Text=He recalled a flash of white hair and cold hands telling him to rest, but little else.
#Text=He thought it best to keep that to himself, however.
#Text=The Knight-Captain didn’t need him spouting off tales of young, nimble Dalish girls traipsing around nearby, especially considering how she’d apparently saved his life.

#Text=If his brain hadn’t been entirely scrambled, then some strange forest dwelling little elven girl had plucked him from a watery grave, nursed him to health for two days and then promptly vanished.
#Text=In all honestly, Cullen had no idea what to think himself.
#Text=“What do you know of Dalish elves, Knight-Captain, ser?”
#Text=He’d nervously asked a few nights after he’d recuperated, as the troops huddled around a steady fire.
#Text=Antoine’s eyes seemed to burn molten red in the flickering light.
#Text=“Why do you ask?
#Text=Have you seen one of them nearby, Rutherford?”
#Text=His voice was all hard edges.
#Text=Cullen frantically shook his head, nerves building in his chest.
#Text=“No!
#Text=No, ser, not at all.
#Text=I merely, wondered….
#Text=They live around the wilderness of the Free Marches, do they not?
#Text=I figured it’s best to be prepared….
#Text=In case of such an event.
#Text=As it were,” he coughed uncomfortably, face heated.
#Text=If the Knight-Captain suspected anything more, he didn’t show it, and Cullen felt himself let out a breath he’d been unconsciously holding.
#Text=He moved to stoke the fire carefully, the crackle and pop of the flames licking the air and filling the silence.
#Text=The other Templars began slowly trailing back to their tents, wishing quiet farewells before trudging off into the dark.

#Text=“The Dalish are not to be trusted, they are wild and do not follow the Maker’s will, ” Antoine muttered after a time, when it was just him and Cullen left around the fading light, his voice low and filled with a bitterness that surprised Cullen.
#Text=“May the Maker turn his gaze on you if you should ever encounter one.”
#Text=He'd heard some tales, of the intensity of hatred that burned within the Dalish people, about their bitterness over a war no one could truly recall specifics of.
#Text=Cullen supposed he would be angry too, if his land and people were stolen.
#Text=They sat in quiet for a while, watching the fire slowly burn out until only embers remained and the pale white of the moon whisked them both into their cots.

#Text=As Cullen pulled off his boots and settled in for the evening amidst other slumbering recruits, he found himself thinking of white hair and pointed ears and a gentle hand tracing patterns through wet hair with a fondness that left him breathless.
#Text=She'd saved his life, she'd thought him worth saving.
#Text=The thought was more comforting than anything he'd experienced as a Templar so far.
#Text=Rush In
#Text=Chapter Summary
#Text=Cullen makes his own path and can no longer stay afloat.
#Text="He should have ran then.
#Text=Antoine's hand clapped down on his shoulder, “Today, we have sent a message.
#Text=That the Maker stands against those who would live against His will.
#Text=That He will not tolerate sinful creatures thieving in the night.
#Text=If the knife ears will not comply, if they will not leave the area and forfeit their goods, and our captured Templar’s to His name and to the Chantry, we will execute this creature.”
#Text=He paused, and a rumble of agreement filled the air.
#Text=“If they will not comply, tomorrow, we are at war.”
#Text=Cullen's voice roared along with his comrades, fists pounding the air, and a black, bitterness seeping into his soul.
#Text=Chapter End Notes
#Text=I figured I should post as much as I can until school swallows me whole, so I apologize if the next update takes a little longer!
#Text=Thanks for sticking with me here, I know the beginning has been pretty slow and all that, but I do have a plan.
#Text=Thank you also for your support, I hope this cliff hanger isn't too mean but honestly?
#Text=I couldn't resist.
#Text=If you notice I did change the summary and the tags, I realized that being mysterious and vague wasn't really worth the confusion.
#Text=Plus slow burn is always more entertaining ;) Anyways!
#Text=Hope you enjoyed!
#Text=EDIT: sorry guys I'm really having a hard time finishing this one here- I think the plot kinda got away from me?
#Text=If there are ppl who really want me to update, leave me a comment or a message or smthn and i'll do my best but otherwise it might take me a while.
#Text=I'm like 3/4ths done the next chapter so I'll try to at least post that one!
